question.text: The Government of India established a 'Fund of Funds' of INR 10,000 crore for start-ups, managed by:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The Government of India announced a Fund of Funds of INR 10,000 crore for start-ups under DPIIT, managed by SIDBI (Small Industries Development Bank of India). This fund invests in SEBI-registered AIFs which in turn invest in start-ups, acting as an enabler for private capital. **The Trap:** SEBI regulates AIFs but doesn't manage the Fund of Funds. SIDBI was chosen because of its mandate to promote small industries and start-ups. **Cross-Exam Context:** Government initiatives supporting the AIF ecosystem are tested in XIX-A. SIDBI's role in the start-up ecosystem is also relevant for Series X-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The government-backed Fund of Funds provides an additional layer of credibility to VCFs that receive SIDBI backing. When pitching VCF investments, mention whether the fund has SIDBI Fund of Funds support as it signals government confidence in the fund's strategy.
